name: Meeting Memory Sync
command:
Step 0: Find the meetings database. Run:
for db in ~/PAPR/jobs/*/data/data.db; do sqlite3 "$db" "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE name='meetings'" 2>/dev/null | grep -q meetings && echo "$db" && break; done
Store the output path as DB_PATH. Use this path for ALL sqlite3 commands below.

Sync completed meetings to PAPR Memory with structured metadata. Follow these steps:

STEP 1: Find un-synced meetings
Run: sqlite3 DB_PATH "SELECT m.id, m.title, m.date, m.summary, m.tags, ce.attendees, ce.start_time, ce.end_time, ce.title as cal_title FROM meetings m LEFT JOIN calendar_events ce ON ce.meeting_id = m.id WHERE m.summary IS NOT NULL AND m.summary != '' AND m.status != 'synced' ORDER BY m.date DESC LIMIT 5"

If no results, print "No meetings to sync" and stop.

STEP 2: For each meeting, store TWO types of memory items using add_agent_memory:

A) MEETING MEMORY — store the full meeting record:
- content: Include meeting title, date, time, all participant names and emails, the FULL summary, key decisions, action items, and follow-ups
- category: "fact", role: "assistant"  
- topics: extract 3-5 key topics from the summary + meeting title
- customMetadata MUST include:
  - memory_type: "meeting"
  - meeting_title: the title
  - meeting_date: YYYY-MM-DD format
  - meeting_time: HH:MM format
  - participants: comma-separated names
  - participant_emails: comma-separated emails
  - meeting_type: "recurring_standup" or "recurring_weekly" or "one_off" or "group_session" (infer from title)
  - tags: from the tags column
  - has_action_items: "true" or "false"

B) PERSON MEMORIES — for each unique attendee NOT ending in @papr.ai, store a person memory:
- content: "Person: NAME (EMAIL) at COMPANY. Participated in MEETING_TITLE on DATE. Context from meeting: [relevant 1-2 sentences about what they discussed or contributed]"
- category: "fact", role: "assistant"
- topics: person name, company name, meeting title
- customMetadata: memory_type: "person", person_name, person_email, person_company (from email domain), meetings_attended

STEP 3: Mark meetings as synced
Run: sqlite3 DB_PATH "UPDATE meetings SET status='synced' WHERE id='MEETING_ID'"

STEP 4: Print summary of what was synced.
